## Current State
- Coverage: 0.0%
- Purpose: Command palette TUI with fuzzy search and animations

## Test Approach
Bubble Tea models are tested by:
1. Init() - verify initial state
2. Update(msg) - verify state transitions
3. View() - verify output contains expected elements

TUI tests are harder to make deterministic - target 60% coverage.

## Test Cases

### model.go

#### Initialization
- [ ] TestNewModel - creates model with default state
- [ ] TestNewModelWithCommands - loads commands correctly
- [ ] TestInitialPhase - starts in PhaseCommand

#### Phase Transitions
- [ ] TestPhaseCommandToTarget - selecting command moves to target phase
- [ ] TestPhaseTargetToConfirm - selecting target sends and exits
- [ ] TestPhaseBack - Escape returns to previous phase

#### Command Filtering
- [ ] TestFilterByLabel - fuzzy match on label
- [ ] TestFilterByKey - fuzzy match on key
- [ ] TestFilterByCategory - fuzzy match on category
- [ ] TestFilterClearOnBackspace - clearing filter shows all
- [ ] TestFilterNoMatches - empty results handled

#### Navigation
- [ ] TestNavigateDown - j/down moves selection
- [ ] TestNavigateUp - k/up moves selection
- [ ] TestNavigateWrap - wraps at boundaries
- [ ] TestQuickSelect - 1-9 selects item

#### Target Selection
- [ ] TestTargetAll - selects all agents
- [ ] TestTargetClaude - selects Claude only
- [ ] TestTargetCodex - selects Codex only
- [ ] TestTargetGemini - selects Gemini only

### Animation (lower priority)
- [ ] TestShimmerEffect - animates on tick
- [ ] TestAnimationTick - tick updates animation state

## View Output Tests
- [ ] TestViewContainsBanner - banner rendered
- [ ] TestViewContainsCommands - command list rendered
- [ ] TestViewContainsPreview - preview pane rendered
- [ ] TestViewContainsKeyHints - keyboard hints shown

## Implementation Notes
- Use tea.Batch for message simulation
- Capture View() output and assert contains
- Animation tests may be flaky, mark appropriately","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleCreek","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:42.477205-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:36:42.584218-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0qz","depends_on_id":"ntm-idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:22:34.045222-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-0wf","title":"E2E Test: Robot Mode JSON Output","description":"# E2E Test: Robot Mode JSON Output

## Scenario
Verify all --robot-* flags produce valid JSON that AI agents can consume.

## Test Cases

### Test 1: --robot-status
- Create various session states
- Run ntm --robot-status
- Parse JSON
- Verify all fields present and accurate
- Log full JSON output

### Test 2: --robot-plan
- Create sessions with different states
- Run ntm --robot-plan
- Verify suggested actions make sense
- Log recommendations

### Test 3: --robot-version
- Run ntm --robot-version
- Verify version/commit/date fields
- Log version info

### Test 4: --robot-help
- Run ntm --robot-help
- Verify documentation present
- Log help content

## Schema Validation
Define and validate JSON schemas:
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["generated_at", "sessions", "summary"],
  "properties": {
    "generated_at": {"type": "string", "format": "date-time"},
    "sessions": {"type": "array"},
    "summary": {"type": "object"}
  }
}
```

## Logging
Include full JSON output in logs for debugging.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeCreek","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:59.020495-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:29:38.950855-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0wf","depends_on_id":"ntm-66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:42.235374-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-0wf","depends_on_id":"ntm-vr2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:43.313959-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-161","title":"Implement ntm mail inbox command","description":"# Implement ntm mail inbox Command

## Purpose

Display an AGGREGATE inbox view showing messages across ALL agents in the project, not just NTM's coordinator messages.

## IMPORTANT: Aggregate View

This is not just NTM's inbox. It shows:
1. Messages TO any agent in the project (GreenCastle, BlueLake, etc.)
2. Messages FROM any agent in the project
3. Filtered by session agents if desired

This gives the user visibility into all agent-to-agent communication.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Show all messages in project (across all agents)
ntm mail inbox myproject

# Filter to session's agents only
ntm mail inbox myproject --session-agents

# Filter to specific agent
ntm mail inbox myproject --agent GreenCastle

# Urgent only
ntm mail inbox myproject --urgent

# JSON for scripts
ntm mail inbox myproject --json
```

## Output Format

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Project Inbox: myproject (5 agents)                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● #42 [URGENT] API Review Needed                            │
│   GreenCastle → BlueLake                                    │
│   2 minutes ago │ Thread: FEAT-123 │ ⚠️ Ack required         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ○ #41 Tests passing, ready for merge                        │
│   RedStone → GreenCastle, BlueLake                          │
│   15 minutes ago │ Thread: FEAT-123                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ○ #40 Starting work on src/api/**                           │
│   ntm_myproject → all                                       │
│   30 minutes ago │ (coordinator announcement)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Showing messages involving: GreenCastle, BlueLake, RedStone, ntm_myproject
```

## Implementation

1. Discover all agents in project via Agent Mail
2. Fetch inbox for EACH agent
3. Deduplicate (same message may appear in multiple inboxes)
4. Sort by timestamp
5. Display with sender → recipient format

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows messages across ALL project agents
- [ ] Deduplication (message shown once even if multiple recipients)
- [ ] Filter by --session-agents, --agent, --urgent
- [ ] Clear indication of sender → recipient
- [ ] Coordinator messages distinguished from agent messages
- [ ] --json output for scripts","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:48.350455-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:01:19.314483-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-161","depends_on_id":"ntm-yxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.9288-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -258,7 +258,7 @@
 {"id":"ntm-sim","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot for unified state query","description":"Core orchestration primitive: single command returns complete system state including all sessions, agent health, bead summary, mail status, and alerts. This is THE most important flag because it eliminates the need for multiple queries.

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",
    "agents": [{
      "pane": "window.pane",
      "type": "claude|codex|gemini|cursor|windsurf|aider",
      "state": "active|idle|stuck|waiting",
      "last_output_age_sec": number,
      "output_tail_lines": number,
      "current_bead": "bead-id|null",
      "pending_mail": number
    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

## Integration Points
- tmux: session/pane enumeration, output capture
- beads: bd list --json integration  
- agent-mail: inbox count per agent
- pane title parsing for agent type detection","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:34.255328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:34.255328-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"ntm-sim","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Implementation Architecture

### File Structure
```
internal/robot/
├── robot.go          # Existing - add Snapshot type and function
├── snapshot.go       # NEW - SnapshotOutput, BuildSnapshot()
├── integrations.go   # NEW - BeadsIntegration, MailIntegration
└── alerts.go         # NEW - AlertManager, Alert types
```

### Core Type
```go
type SnapshotOutput struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time              `json:"ts"`
    Sessions    []SessionSnapshot      `json:"sessions"`
    Beads       *BeadsSnapshot         `json:"beads,omitempty"`
    AgentMail   *MailSnapshot          `json:"agent_mail,omitempty"`
    Alerts      []Alert                `json:"alerts"`
    Summary     SnapshotSummary        `json:"summary"`
}
```

### Flag Registration
In root.go init():
```go
rootCmd.Flags().Bool("robot-snapshot", false, "Output complete system state as JSON")
rootCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Return only changes since timestamp (ISO8601)")
```

### Performance Target
- Cold snapshot: <100ms
- With caching: <20ms
- Memory: <5MB for 10 sessions with 50 panes","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:34Z"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-t0ch","title":"Implement file conflict detection","description":"# Implement File Conflict Detection

## Purpose

Detect when multiple agents modify the same file, potentially creating conflicts.

## Detection Logic

```go
type Conflict struct {
    Path   string
    Agents []AgentChange
}

func DetectConflicts(changes []AgentChange) []Conflict {
    byPath := make(map[string][]AgentChange)
    for _, c := range changes {
        byPath[c.File] = append(byPath[c.File], c)
    }
    
    var conflicts []Conflict
    for path, agents := range byPath {
        if len(agents) > 1 {
            conflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{
                Path:   path,
                Agents: agents,
            })
        }
    }
    return conflicts
}
```

## Severity Levels

- **Warning**: Same file modified by 2+ agents (may or may not conflict)
- **Critical**: Same lines modified (if we can detect via git diff)

## Integration with ntm send --all

After broadcast completes:
```
ntm send myproject --all "Implement the feature"
# ... agents work ...
# On completion:
⚠️ Potential conflict: src/api/users.py modified by cc_1, cc_2
  Run `ntm conflicts myproject` for details
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detect same-file modifications across agents
- [ ] Warn user after broadcasts
- [ ] ntm conflicts command shows details
- [ ] Severity classification (warning vs critical)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:14.387332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T20:22:25.403304-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-t0ch","depends_on_id":"ntm-xkvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:37.60962-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-t0ch","title":"Implement file conflict detection","description":"# Implement File Conflict Detection

## Purpose

Detect when multiple agents modify the same file, potentially creating conflicts.

## Detection Logic

```go
type Conflict struct {
    Path   string
    Agents []AgentChange
}

func DetectConflicts(changes []AgentChange) []Conflict {
    byPath := make(map[string][]AgentChange)
    for _, c := range changes {
        byPath[c.File] = append(byPath[c.File], c)
    }
    
    var conflicts []Conflict
    for path, agents := range byPath {
        if len(agents) > 1 {
            conflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{
                Path:   path,
                Agents: agents,
            })
        }
    }
    return conflicts
}
```

## Severity Levels

- **Warning**: Same file modified by 2+ agents (may or may not conflict)
- **Critical**: Same lines modified (if we can detect via git diff)

## Integration with ntm send --all

After broadcast completes:
```
ntm send myproject --all "Implement the feature"
# ... agents work ...
# On completion:
⚠️ Potential conflict: src/api/users.py modified by cc_1, cc_2
  Run `ntm conflicts myproject` for details
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detect same-file modifications across agents
- [ ] Warn user after broadcasts
- [ ] ntm conflicts command shows details

 {"id":"ntm-t7ol","title":"Phase 3: Auto-Trigger on Rate Limit Detection","description":"# Phase 3: Auto-Trigger on Rate Limit Detection

## What This Is
Automatically trigger rotation assistance when a rate limit is detected, instead of requiring user to manually run `ntm rotate`.

## Leverages Existing Infrastructure
NTM already has rate limit detection in `internal/health/health.go`:
```go
var errorPatterns = []struct {
    Pattern *regexp.Regexp
    Type    string
    Message string
}{
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)rate.?limit`), "rate_limit", "Rate limit detected"},
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)429`), "rate_limit", "HTTP 429 rate limit"},
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)too.?many.?requests`), "rate_limit", "Too many requests"},
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)quota.?exceeded`), "rate_limit", "Quota exceeded"},
}
```

And notification in `internal/notify/notify.go`:
```go
EventRateLimit EventType = "agent.rate_limit"
```

We just need to **connect** limit detection → rotation trigger.

## Implementation Approach

### Option A: Background Monitor (Recommended)
Add rotation trigger to the resilience monitor:

```go
// In internal/resilience/monitor.go

func (m *Monitor) handleRateLimit(agent *AgentState, waitSeconds int) {
    // Existing notification
    if m.cfg.RateLimit.Notify {
        m.notifier.Notify(notify.NewRateLimitEvent(...))
    }
    
    // NEW: Trigger rotation assistance
    if m.cfg.Rotation.AutoTrigger {
        m.triggerRotationAssistance(agent)
    }
}

func (m *Monitor) triggerRotationAssistance(agent *AgentState) {
    // Send desktop notification with action hint
    m.notifier.Notify(notify.Event{
        Type:    notify.EventRotationNeeded,
        Message: fmt.Sprintf("Rate limit hit! Run: ntm rotate %s --pane=%d", m.session, agent.PaneIndex),
    })
    
    // Optionally auto-initiate rotation (if configured)
    if m.cfg.Rotation.AutoInitiate {
        // Start the rotation flow in a separate goroutine
        go m.initiateRotation(agent)
    }
}
```

### Option B: Dashboard Integration
When dashboard is open and detects limit, show prominent alert:

```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  RATE LIMIT DETECTED on pane 0 (Claude)                   ║
║                                                               ║
║  Press 'r' to rotate account, or run: ntm rotate --pane=0     ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Option C: tmux Alert (Simplest)
When running `ntm spawn` with monitoring, show tmux message:

```go
func alertRateLimit(session string, paneIdx int) {
    msg := fmt.Sprintf("Rate limit! Run: ntm rotate %s --pane=%d", session, paneIdx)
    exec.Command("tmux", "display-message", "-d", "10000", msg).Run()
}
```

## Configuration
```toml
[rotation]
# Existing fields...
auto_trigger = true       # Show notification when limit detected
auto_initiate = false     # Actually start rotation automatically (aggressive)
```

## What This Builds On
- Phase 1: Core rotation command
- Existing health detection
- Existing notification system

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Desktop notification when rate limit detected
- [ ] Notification includes command to run
- [ ] Dashboard shows alert (if open)
- [ ] Optional auto-initiation (disabled by default)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:14:52.846787-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T17:39:00.21314-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T17:39:00.21314-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-t7ol","depends_on_id":"ntm-cs4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:01.91758-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-uwa","title":"Implement Agent Mail server detection and config","description":"# Implement Agent Mail Server Detection and Config

## Purpose

Before using any Agent Mail features, NTM needs to detect if the server is running and load configuration.

## Configuration Schema

Add to ~/.config/ntm/config.toml:
```toml
[agent_mail]
enabled = true                              # Master toggle
url = "http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/"          # Server endpoint
token = ""                                  # Bearer token (or use env var)
auto_register = true                        # Auto-register sessions as agents
program_name = "ntm"                        # Program identifier for registration
```

Environment variable overrides:
- AGENT_MAIL_URL
- AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN
- AGENT_MAIL_ENABLED (0/1)

## Detection Logic

```go
func (c *Client) IsAvailable() bool {
    // 1. Check if enabled in config
    // 2. Attempt health_check() with 2s timeout
    // 3. Cache result for 30s to avoid repeated checks
}

func (c *Client) RequireAvailable() error {
    if !c.IsAvailable() {
        return ErrAgentMailUnavailable
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **ntm deps**: Show Agent Mail status
   ```
   Agent Mail: ✓ running (http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/)
   Agent Mail: ✗ not detected (optional)
   ```

2. **Config loading**: internal/config loads agent_mail section
3. **Lazy initialization**: Client created on first use, not at startup
4. **Status command**: Show connection status in ntm status

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config schema added with sensible defaults
- [ ] Environment variables override config
- [ ] Detection is fast (<2s timeout) and cached
- [ ] ntm deps shows Agent Mail status
- [ ] Graceful behavior when server unavailable","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:01.67162-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:32:32.28481-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:32:32.28481-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-uwa","depends_on_id":"ntm-ceb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.651713-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-vr2","title":"SUB-EPIC: E2E Tests with Detailed Logging","description":"# SUB-EPIC: End-to-End (E2E) Tests with Detailed Logging

## Scope
Complete workflow tests that exercise NTM from CLI invocation to tmux state verification.

## E2E Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Full Agent Workflow
1. ntm spawn project --cc=2 --cod=1
2. Verify session created with 4 panes (3 agents + 1 user)
3. ntm send project --cc 'hello world'
4. Verify prompt delivered to Claude panes only
5. ntm interrupt project
6. Verify Ctrl+C sent
7. ntm kill -f project
8. Verify session destroyed

### Scenario 2: Command Palette Workflow
1. Create session with agents
2. Invoke palette (programmatic, not TUI)
3. Select command
4. Verify prompt sent to targets

### Scenario 3: Quick Setup Workflow
1. ntm quick testproject --template=go
2. Verify directory structure created
3. Verify git initialized
4. Verify .vscode and .claude dirs created

### Scenario 4: Session Persistence
1. Create session, add agents
2. Detach
3. Re-attach
4. Verify state preserved

### Scenario 5: Robot Mode Output
1. Create various session states
2. Run --robot-status, --robot-plan
3. Verify JSON output matches actual state
4. Parse and validate JSON schema

## Logging Requirements

### Per-Test Log File
```
test_logs/
├── TestFullAgentWorkflow_20251210_103045.log
├── TestCommandPaletteWorkflow_20251210_103047.log
└── ...
```

### Log Content Structure
```
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] TEST START: TestFullAgentWorkflow
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] SETUP: Creating test session 'ntm_test_abc123'
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] EXEC: ntm spawn ntm_test_abc123 --cc=2 --cod=1
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] STDOUT: Session created: ntm_test_abc123 (4 panes)
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] VERIFY: Checking pane count...
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] ASSERT PASS: pane_count == 4
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z] EXEC: ntm send ntm_test_abc123 --cc 'hello'
...
[2025-12-10T10:30:48Z] TEARDOWN: Killing session ntm_test_abc123
[2025-12-10T10:30:48Z] TEST END: PASS (3.2s)
```

### System State Capture
Before and after each test:
- tmux list-sessions
- tmux list-panes (for test session)
- Environment variables
- Working directory

## Implementation
- tests/e2e/ directory for all E2E tests
- Shared test harness with logging
- CI-compatible (skip if tmux unavailable)
- Parallel execution with unique session names","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BrownPond","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:52.553331-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T20:29:40.739937-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-vr2","depends_on_id":"ntm-l5h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:06.044553-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-vr2","depends_on_id":"ntm-7lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:06.232148-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-xle","title":"Implement cross-platform clipboard detection and access","description":"Create internal/clipboard package for cross-platform clipboard access.

## What This Task Does

Create a clipboard abstraction that works on macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland),
and WSL. This provides the foundation for clipboard-related features.

## Platform Detection

Detect platform and available clipboard tools:

macOS:
- pbcopy / pbpaste (always available)

Linux X11:
- xclip -selection clipboard
- xsel --clipboard

Linux Wayland:
- wl-copy / wl-paste

WSL:
- clip.exe (write only)
- powershell.exe Get-Clipboard (read)

Fallback:
- tmux's internal clipboard (with warning about limitations)

## Implementation

package clipboard

type Clipboard interface {
    Copy(content string) error
    Paste() (string, error)
    Available() bool
}

func New() (Clipboard, error) {
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "darwin":
        return &macOSClipboard{}, nil
    case "linux":
        if isWSL() {
            return &wslClipboard{}, nil
        }
        if isWayland() {
            return &waylandClipboard{}, nil
        }
        return &x11Clipboard{}, nil
    }
    return &tmuxClipboard{}, nil
}

## Error Handling

- Tool not found: Clear error with installation suggestion
- Permission denied: Suggest running with appropriate access
- Empty clipboard: Return empty string, not error

## Testing

Need to handle:
- CI environments (no display, no clipboard)
- SSH sessions (no local clipboard)
- Container environments

Skip clipboard tests or mock in these environments.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clipboard works on macOS
- [ ] Clipboard works on Linux X11
- [ ] Clipboard works on Linux Wayland
- [ ] Clipboard works on WSL
- [ ] Graceful fallback when tools missing
- [ ] Clear error messages

 {"id":"ntm-y44","title":"EPIC: Advanced Robot Orchestration Flags","description":"Comprehensive robot mode flags for AI orchestrator control. Enables unified state queries, delta updates, batch operations, and cross-tool correlation. Priority based on orchestration value.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:49:44.994993-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T20:26:49.899514-05:00","comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"ntm-y44","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Orchestrator's Perspective: Why These Flags Matter

This epic represents a fundamental shift in how NTM serves AI orchestrators. The existing --robot-status flag is just the beginning. Here's the complete vision:

### Core Philosophy
An AI orchestrator managing 10+ agents across multiple sessions needs:
1. **Complete situational awareness** in a single query
2. **Token efficiency** to maximize useful work vs overhead
3. **Fast feedback loops** for responsive orchestration
4. **Cross-tool correlation** (tmux ↔ beads ↔ agent-mail)

### The Token Economics
Consider orchestrating 8 agents:
- Current approach: 8+ tool calls, ~2000 tokens of output to parse
- With --robot-snapshot: 1 call, ~500 tokens, everything I need
- With --since delta: 1 call, ~50 tokens for incremental updates

This is a 40x improvement in polling scenarios.

### Dependency Structure
The implementation follows a logical build-up:
1. **Foundation**: Agent detection (ntm-3n9) + Alert system (ntm-rlp)
2. **Integrations**: Agent-mail (ntm-gk5) + Beads (ntm-l5y)
3. **Core**: Unified snapshot (ntm-sim)
4. **Extensions**: Delta mode (ntm-dvz), Terse (ntm-oba), Health (ntm-c36)
5. **Advanced**: Graph (ntm-i5x), Assign (ntm-20n), Interrupt (ntm-pb3)

### Success Criteria
- Single --robot-snapshot call provides complete state
- Delta queries return only changes (95% token savings)
- All output is structured JSON, no parsing human text
- Cross-tool correlation works (know which agent → which bead → which thread)
- Batch operations support multi-agent commands","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:21Z"},{"id":13,"issue_id":"ntm-y44","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Revised Architecture: Minimal Dependencies

After review, the dependency structure has been revised. Core robot flags should work IMMEDIATELY without requiring integrations. Integrations ENHANCE but don't BLOCK.

### Tier 1: Core (No blockers - implement first)
- **ntm-sim**: --robot-snapshot - unified state query
- **ntm-ign**: --robot-tail - multi-pane output
- **ntm-d2t**: --robot-send - batch messaging  
- **ntm-c36**: --robot-health - health matrix
- **ntm-oln**: --robot-spawn - structured session creation
- **ntm-pb3**: --robot-interrupt - priority course correction

### Tier 2: Extensions (depend on Tier 1)
- **ntm-dvz**: --robot-snapshot --since - delta updates (needs ntm-sim + ntm-hat)
- **ntm-oba**: --robot-terse - compact output (needs ntm-sim)
- **ntm-a8q**: --robot-ack - send confirmation

### Tier 3: Enhancements (enhance Tier 1 but don't block)
- **ntm-3n9**: Agent detection (improves accuracy of all queries)
- **ntm-rlp**: Alert generation (adds alerts to snapshot/health)
- **ntm-gk5**: Agent-mail integration (adds mail data to snapshot)
- **ntm-l5y**: Beads integration (adds bead data to snapshot)
- **ntm-hat**: State tracking ring buffer (enables delta mode)

### Tier 4: Advanced Features
- **ntm-20n**: --robot-assign - work distribution recommendations
- **ntm-i5x**: --robot-graph - cross-tool correlation
- **ntm-sc6**: Progress detection
- **ntm-fpb**: Command history tracking
- **ntm-lla**: Session save/restore

### Implementation Order Recommendation
1. Core Tier 1 flags (can be implemented in parallel)
2. State tracking (ntm-hat) for delta support
3. Delta mode (ntm-dvz) 
4. Enhancements as needed
5. Advanced features based on user demand","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:59:37Z"},{"id":14,"issue_id":"ntm-y44","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Child Tasks

This epic tracks the following tasks:

### Core Robot Flags (Tier 1 - No blockers)
- ntm-sim: --robot-snapshot (unified state query)
- ntm-ign: --robot-tail (multi-pane output)
- ntm-d2t: --robot-send (batch messaging)

### Extensions (Tier 2)

### Enhancements (Tier 3)

 {"id":"ntm-y7l","title":"Implement agent tagging in pane titles","description":"Store and parse agent tags in tmux pane titles.

## What This Task Does

Extend the pane title format to include user-defined tags:
- Current: session__cc_1
- New: session__cc_1[frontend,refactor]

## Tag Storage Format

Tags are comma-separated within square brackets at the end of pane title.

Examples:
- myproject__cc_1[frontend]
- myproject__cod_1[backend,api,urgent]
- myproject__gmi_1[] (no tags, explicit empty)

## Implementation

Add to internal/tmux package:

func SetPaneTags(session string, paneIndex int, tags []string) error {
    currentTitle := GetPaneTitle(session, paneIndex)
    baseTitle := stripTags(currentTitle)
    newTitle := baseTitle + "[" + strings.Join(tags, ",") + "]"
    return exec.Command("tmux", "select-pane", "-t", target, "-T", newTitle).Run()
}

func GetPaneTags(session string, paneIndex int) []string {
    title := GetPaneTitle(session, paneIndex)
    // Parse [tag1,tag2] from end of title
    ...
}

## Integration with spawn/add

$ ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --tags=frontend
# Creates: myproject__cc_1[frontend], myproject__cc_2[frontend]

$ ntm add myproject --cod=1 --tags=backend,api
# Creates: myproject__cod_1[backend,api]

## Tag modification

$ ntm tag myproject:1 --add=urgent
$ ntm tag myproject:1 --remove=frontend
$ ntm tag myproject:1 --set=backend,api

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tags stored in pane title
- [ ] Tags parsed correctly from title
- [ ] --tags flag works on spawn command
- [ ] --tags flag works on add command
- [ ] ntm tag command for modification
- [ ] Tags survive session detach/reattach
